tax: Placed at genus rank, Whirler is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a spinning shaft-air column body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a spinning air-column body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dust-marked spinning column. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — upward-spiralling dust — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Whirler occupies vertical shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure spins a draught and recur with the draught resumes, sustaining themselves on channelled draught.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: flings crews from ledges and ropes. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: spinning column and air-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: flinging, lifting, and sudden stilling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Whirler matters because flings crews from ledges. It lets survey reports sort spinning-column forms from pooling ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Whirler is the genus-rank grouping of contained whirlwind elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a spinning shaft-air column that flings crews and debris, and divide below into species groups by shaft height and spin force. An invisible whirlwind bounded to a shaft.
